title: Gathering the Mind Before Work
intention: To gather scattered attention into calm, available alertness before a stretch of work.

lines.text: Welcome. Sit down, and let yourself simply be here.
Set both feet on the floor. Feel the ground meet you, steady and indifferent.
Let the breath come in, and let it go.
Notice where the mind has scattered. Some of it is reaching toward what hasn't happened yet.
Some things ahead are up to you. Many are not.
Set down what is not yours to carry. You will meet it when it comes, and not before.
What remains in your hands is this: your attention, your effort, your judgment.
These are enough. They are all that was ever asked of you.
Take a slower breath now, and gather the scattered pieces inward.
Let the next breath be ordinary. Just follow it, without improving it.
Feel the body grow quiet, but not heavy. Awake, and willing.
The task ahead is one thing, done with care. That is the whole of it.
Bring your eyes to a single point, or let them rest closed and clear.
You are present. You are unhurried. You are ready.
Take one full breath, and let alertness fill the body like light.
Now begin, and meet what is in front of you.
